result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would eradicate the deep-seated belief that some people are worth more than others.  

This isn't about a single policy or technology, but about dismantling the fundamental mindset that allows us to accept inequality—whether based on race, nationality, wealth, caste, gender, or circumstance. It’s the root of so much suffering: systemic injustice, exploitation of people and the planet, and the quiet, everyday cruelties of indifference.  

If that core perception shifted, everything else would realign. Systems would be built on dignity rather than dominance. Resources would be shared out of solidarity, not scarcity-driven competition. And "progress" would be measured by the well-being of the most vulnerable, not by the accumulation of the most powerful.  

In short, I would change the *lens* through which we see each other—from one of separation and hierarchy to one of shared humanity and inherent worth.
